violations.text: Bean Sprouts were observed held at 49 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Ice was added to bean sprouts to blast chill. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026

The screen door to the outside, located in the rear, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026
Grout surrounding floor tiles near dish machine was observed worn down (causing water accumulation). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2026

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026

There was no evidence that employees are required to report to the person in charge information about their health (illnesses, symptoms) as they relate to diseases that are transmissible through food. State example provided during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. State guidance provided during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026

Raw shrimp, raw beef, and raw chicken were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods such as vegetables, cooked shrimp, and noodles in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination (raw chicken over raw beef). Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026

Food contact surfaces of slicer, meat slicer machine, robot coupe food processor and grinder were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Equipment was cleaned and sanitized during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026

Bean sprouts in water over ice were observed held at 56 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Ice was added to bean sprouts and water to blast chill. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026

The screen door to the outside, located in the rear, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026
Boxes of single service items were observed on the floor in storage room. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026
Perforated steam hotel pans were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026
Rubber molding on slicer was observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2026
Gaskets for 2 door reach-in were observed in poor repair. Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2026
Dumpster for trash was observed missing side doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026
Openings were observed by food prep sink and back hallway storage area. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026

The walls and coving surrounding equipment area have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026
Grout surrounding floor tiles near dish machine was observed worn down (causing water accumulation). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2026

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the dumpster area and storage hallway. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the dry storage area with food and single service items. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 04/13/2026

Beef, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Beef was observed at 46 DF and was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/18/2025

Prep unit #3 was observed held at 47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, almost all food was just placed in unit prior to inspection. Food was observed held between 36-46 DF. Food that was in temperature, and food that was just placed in unit was moved to working refrigeration. Food that was in unit overnight was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Establishment is not to store any TCS foods in unit until unit is able to hold an ambient temperature of 41 DF or less. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/18/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, noodles were placed covered and hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Noodles were uncovered and placed on sheet pan at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/18/2025
Various food items were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025
Prep unit #3 gasket was observed in poor repair. Gaskets must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/19/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. RIFSM has expired. Establishment must re-apply for a RIFSM. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils ice machine and can opener were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener was cleaned at the time of inspection. Ice machine was emptied and put out of service at the time of the inspection. Establishment is not to use ice machine until it is properly cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

Prep unit #3 was observed held at 50 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Some food was just placed in unit and moved to properly functioning refrigeration. Various other foods were observed between 46-58 DF. All food above 44 DF was voluntarily disposed of. Establishment is not to store any TCS foods in unit until unit is able to hold an ambient temperature of 41 DF or lower. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

Various foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, chicken wings were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Chicken wings were moved to sheet pans at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

ROP crab meat was observed with ROP packaging still intact. ROP was observed at 48 DF in prep unit #3 and was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Proper thawing reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

The door to the outside, located in the back of kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025
Various food items were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025
Prep unit #3 gasket was observed in poor repair. Gaskets must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/19/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dishwasher. Establishment is to obtain test strips. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2025
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. Toilet rooms must be self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 06/19/2025

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arrranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrective Action - No cross contamination was observed during time of inspection, food items were correctly stored. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2024
Food items were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen and walk in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2024

The cutting boards in the kitchen are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024
The ice well is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2024
The door to the toilet room is not self-closing. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2024
